title: 3XFlag-pATn5 Protein Purification and MEDS-loading (5x scale, 2L volume)
author: Terri Bryson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8yrhxv6
materials: Roche Complete EDTA free protease inhibitor (PI) tablets

steps.title: Streak E. coli
steps.instruction: Streak E. coli glycerol stock containing pTXB1-rbs_3XFlag-pATn5-FL plasmid onto an LB-Amp 100 plate, incubate Overnight at 37 °C

steps.title: Make a patch plate
steps.instruction: Select a single colony and make a patch plate for later, use within 1 week.

steps.title: Inoculate starter culture
steps.instruction: Early morning, with a sterile toothpick swipe through a bacterial patch to inoculate 30 mL of LB broth+ carbenicillin 100ug/mL culture, let shake ~ 04:00:00 .

steps.title: Prepare large volume media
steps.instruction: Put 1970 mL of LB+ carbenicillin 100ug/mL in 6L flask at 37 °C as well so it is already warm at the time of dilution. Carbenicillin can be used in place of ampicillin. Carbenicillin is more stable, so it is potentially more effective at selecting only bacteria containing the plasmids of interest (for example, fewer satellite colonies will grow). However, it is also more expensive.

steps.title: Prepare buffers and media
steps.instruction: Make buffers and media. See Materials section .

steps.title: Inoculate large culture
steps.instruction: After 04:00:00 incubation use all 30 ml of starter culture to inoculate larger vessel.

steps.title: Grow culture
steps.instruction: Shake at 37 °C , check OD600 periodically until it reaches O.D600 of 0.5-0.7. ~ 03:00:00

steps.title: Cool culture
steps.instruction: Move to cold room to let cool ~ 01:00:00

steps.title: Induce protein expression
steps.instruction: Induce culture with addition of fresh 1M IPTG to 0.25mM final and incubate on the 18 °C shaker ~ Overnight

steps.title: Pellet bacteria
steps.instruction: Pellet bacteria by centrifugation: Divide the culture between 6x 500 mL bottles, centrifuge at 9500rcf, 4C for 01:00:00 in a GS3 Rotor, RC5C Sorvall centrifuge.

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Freeze bacterial pellets
steps.instruction: Freeze bacterial pellets in dry ice and ethanol bath.

steps.title: Store pellets
steps.instruction: Store at -80 °C freezer until ready to process.

steps.title: Thaw and resuspend pellets
steps.instruction: Need 400 mL, HEGX+PI today; make a total of 1L HEGX and add PI tablets as needed each day. Remove pellets from freezer, thaw on ice and resuspend together in a combined total of 200 mL of chilled HEGX Buffer with Roche Complete EDTA free protease inhibitor (PI) tablets. Keep on ice while working.

steps.title: Divide resuspension for sonication
steps.instruction: Divide combined resuspension between 4x clean 100 ml plastic beakers nested in a larger plastic beaker filled with ice. See figure A.

steps.title: Sonicate samples
steps.instruction: While keeping in the ice nest, sonicate each sample 10-12 times, 00:00:45 each at 50% duty cycle and output level 7 on a Branson Sonifier 250 fitted with the Branson Ultrasonics™ Sonifier™ micro-tip. Between rounds of sonication keep in larger ice bucket (see figure A) and use a 2mL serological pipette to gently stir to facilitate cooling. Let each sample rest at least 00:00:45 between sonication rounds. To prevent foaming keep the sonicator tip in the center of the sample. If excessive foaming occurs during sonication either wait for foam to settle or remove with a pipette and discard.

steps.title: Pellet lysate
steps.instruction: Transfer to fresh 50 mL conical tubes and pellet the lysate by centrifugation for 00:30:00 , 4 °C , at 16000 x g, °C .

steps.title: Prepare Chitin columns
steps.instruction: Chitin Binding: The remainder of the protocol is carried out at 4 °C or on ice. All buffers should be chilled on ice prior to use. In 4 °C, room prepare 10x Econo-Pac® Chromatography Columns (BIO-RAD 732-1010), each holds 20 mL Set-up columns (we use a rack intended for Qiagen Maxi-preps, figure B). Gently invert Chitin Resin (NEB S6651S) to fully suspend and add 2.5mL to each column. Wash each with 20 mL of HEGX Buffer, drain immediately, don’t collect anything. Do not let resin dry out prior to addition of sample.

steps.title: Cap columns
steps.instruction: Add yellow cap to bottom of column.

steps.title: Add supernatant to resin
steps.instruction: Add supernatant from lysate spin to Chitin Resin slowly to prevent bubbles.
